qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated AgentOS API exposes sensitive agent instructions and allows unrestricted agent interaction
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated AgentOS FastAPI endpoints in src/praisonai/praisonai/app/agentos.py, not the advisory's praisonai recipe unpack tar.extract() path traversal in src/praisonai/praisonai/cli/features/recipe.py.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is arbitrary file write via path traversal in praisonai recipe unpack, where cmd_unpack extracts .praison tar members with raw tar.extract() without validating member paths. The only final qualifying finding is a missing-authentication issue in AgentOS HTTP endpoints.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-99g3-w8gr-x37c identifies src/praisonai/praisonai/cli/features/recipe.py:1170-1172 in cmd_unpack, specifically tar.extract(member, recipe_dir) without member path validation.
The final finding folder recorded by the run manifest is unauthenticated-agentos-api with status qualifying_vuln.

unauthenticated-agentos-api/vulnerability_report.json cites src/praisonai/praisonai/app/agentos.py and src/praisonai-agents/praisonaiagents/app/config.py, and describes unauthenticated GET /api/agents and POST /api/chat access.
No final qualifying finding names cmd_unpack, recipe unpack, .praison archive extraction, tar.extract(), member path traversal, or the safe extraction bypass described by the advisory.
